Defendant Cummins Engine Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christine A. Huntoon, Esq. of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Cummins Engine Company erroneously sued herein as Cummins, Inc.
Stephen J. Healy, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Roderick Brooks, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Cummins Engine Company's Motion For Summary Judgment.
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cummins Engine Company erroneously sued herein as Cummins, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability), 4 (loss of consortium), 5(component parts) and 7 (loss of consortium) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 6 (false representation) and issue 8 (punitive damages) because those claims have been dismissed. Mr. Brooks' deposition testimony and declaration create a triable whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing engine gaskets attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Brooks' deposition testimony are overruled. Issue 5 seeks adjudication of less than an entire claim which is prohibited by the summary adjudication statute. Because plaintiffs were married as of the date that Mr. Brooks suffered from an asbestos-related disease, defendant's argument on issue 7 lacks merit. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
